question: Unlike Cranial capacity (which involves the internal measurable volume of the protective braincase inside the skull, widely used as an excellent indicator of evolutionary brain size), which term is specifically characterized by the unique, habitual ability to walk upright strictly on two lower limbs, marking a massive key milestone early in human evolution?
explanation: Bipedalism involves the unique, habitual ability to walk upright strictly on two lower limbs, marking a massive key milestone early in human evolution, whereas Cranial capacity involves the internal measurable volume of the protective braincase inside the skull, widely used as an excellent indicator of evolutionary brain size.
